content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a reflective cognitive practice and the physical reflection of meaning through language, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the language education framework of reflexion to integrate graded, vocabulary-like structured memory schemas for storing past reasoning attempts instead of unstructured raw buffers. This structured 'lexicalized reflection' would let LLMs retrieve refined reasoning chunks the way human learners recall mastered vocabulary, rather than re-evaluating full uncurated solution trajectories every time they self-reflect.
